title: lesquerella gracilis subsp. nuttallii
intro: Lesquerella gracilis subsp. nuttallii is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the mustard family and is native to North America. It is also known as Nuttall's bladderpod, and its roots and leaves have been used in herbal medicine for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, such as skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory problems, and is also valued for its potential to lower cholesterol levels and improve cardiovascular health. In herbal preparations, Lesquerella gracilis subsp. nuttallii is often used in tinctures, salves, and infusions, and is also used as a food ingredient in some traditional recipes.
